other_observers: Nick Komar; The Black Phoebe was originally found sometime in mid-December in the same area.
specific_location: Valco Ponds just beneth the Pueblo Reservoir dam. Where the bird was: walk from the parking area to where you can see the river. A few yards to the east is a path going down to the waters edge, walk east about 75 yards and the bird was on a stick on the North side of the river.
description: It was a medium sized flycatcher. It had a black head, back, breast, and wings. The head was lightly darker. It had an white belly.Behaviors: The bird would sit on a branch and then fly down and catch a bug off the top of the water. It kept doing the tail motion that phoebes do, flick the up and down.Call: It kept giving a high pitched tseewPlumage: Unknown
